title: RWisecondorX Agent Guidelines
text: Build R toolkit copy number analysis trisomy prediction non-invasive prenatal testing (NIPT), comparable scope NIPTUtils built entirely Rduckhts/DuckDB rather Rsamtools/Python. package ports two upstream implementations: WisecondorX — copy number variation detection cfDNA (Python/pysam upstream) NIPTeR — fast trisomy prediction via chromosomal fraction Z-scores, NCV scores, regression, chi-squared correction (R/Rsamtools upstream) Design priorities: Exact conformance upstream implementations documented testable. Performance — DuckDB SQL replaces per-read Python loops Rsamtools scans; large BAMs run seconds, minutes. Interoperable file formats — bgzipped, tabix-indexed BED files intermediate layer binning analysis, consumable DuckDB, R, Python, tabix-aware tool. Python runtime dependency — Rduckhts replaces pysam HTS operations; reticulate condathis optional used conformance testing.

title: RWisecondorX Agent Guidelines
text: Shared binning engine — R/convert.R - bam_convert(): DuckDB/SQL read-counting core. Supports binsize, mapq, require_flags (samtools -f), exclude_flags (samtools -F), rmdup ("streaming" / "flag" / "none"), CRAM via reference. Achieves exact bin--bin conformance WisecondorX HG00106.chrom11 (25,115 non-zero bins, 0 mismatches). - bam_convert_bed(): bgzipped + tabix-indexed 4-column BED output (chrom, start, end, count). Uses Rduckhts::rduckhts_bgzip() rduckhts_tabix_index() — external tools. WisecondorX layer - R/wisecondorx_cli.R: wisecondorx_convert(), wisecondorx_newref(), wisecondorx_predict() — thin condathis wrappers delegating official bioconda package. - R/npz.R: bam_convert_npz() — WisecondorX-compatible NPZ output via reticulate. NIPTeR binning layer — R/nipter_bin.R - nipter_bin_bam(): produces NIPTeRSample objects (class c("NIPTeRSample", "CombinedStrands")). Exposes mapq, require_flags, exclude_flags, rmdup pre-filtering matching real-world NIPT pipelines (e.g. mapq=40L, exclude_flags=1024L). - nipter_bin_bam_bed(): 5-column BED (chrom, start, end, count, corrected_count). corrected_count NA GC correction ported; column present downstream tools can rely schema now. Tests - inst/tinytest/test_fixtures.R (41 assertions): synthetic BAM/CRAM fixtures, three rmdup modes, CRAM reference round-trip. - inst/tinytest/test_nipter.R (47 assertions): NIPTeRSample structure, matrix dimensions rownames, MAPQ filter, exclude_flags=1024 dedup, BED.gz output shape, optional bin--bin conformance NIPTeR::bin_bam_sample() via NIPTER_CONFORMANCE_BAM env var. - inst/tinytest/test_cli_args.R: CLI argument builder unit tests. - inst/tinytest/test_npz.R: NPZ round-trip tests. - inst/tinytest/test_integration.R: WisecondorX bin--bin conformance via condathis. - inst/tinytest/test_sra_metadata.R: SRA URL helpers. Infrastructure - R/aaa.R: SRA metadata helpers. - scripts/make_fixtures.sh: reproducible synthetic BAM/CRAM fixture generation. - DESCRIPTION: NIPTeR authors (Dirk de Weerd, Lennart Johansson) listed cph. NIPTeR added Suggests. NIPTeR statistical layer file strictly separate; never mix NIPTeR WisecondorX code. R/nipter_control.R — nipter_as_control_group(): constructs NIPTeRControlGroup list NIPTeRSample objects validation dedup. nipter_diagnose_control_group(): per-chromosome Z-scores Shapiro-Wilk normality tests across control group. nipter_match_control_group(): selects best-fit control samples test sample sum--squares chromosomal fraction distance. R/nipter_gc.R — nipter_gc_correct(): LOESS bin-weight GC correction. GC content derived FASTA reference via rduckhts_fasta_nuc() — bundled correction tables (unlike original NIPTeR sysdata.rda). Supports single samples control groups. Sex chromosome correction via nearest-neighbour (LOESS) -bucket weights (bin-weight). R/nipter_chi.R — nipter_chi_correct(): chi-squared overdispersion correction applied simultaneously sample control group. Uses normalised chi-squared threshold (default 3.5). Sex chromosome correction uses mean autosomal correction factor per bin position. R/nipter_score.R — nipter_z_score(): chromosomal fraction Z-score Shapiro-Wilk normality test. nipter_ncv_score(): normalised chromosome value brute-force denominator search using utils::combn() (replaces sets::set_combn() dependency). R/nipter_regression.R — nipter_regression(): forward stepwise regression Z-score train/test split, practical vs theoretical CV selection. Currently CombinedStrands ; SeparatedStrands requires separate_strands = TRUE support. inst/tinytest/test_nipter_stats.R — 69 assertions covering statistical functions.

title: RWisecondorX Agent Guidelines
text: separate_strands = TRUE: currently stubbed stop() nipter_bin_bam(). Requires GROUP rname, bin, strand SQL layer returning two matrices (forward_reads_matrix reverse_reads_matrix). Needed SeparatedStrands variant nipter_regression(), already implemented structurally gated input class. Gender/sex prediction: investigate whether use NIPTeR’s Y-fraction method, WisecondorX’s --yfrac approach, unified strategy. DNACopy replacement: segmentation step wisecondorx_predict uses DNACopy internally; evaluate whether expose replace . Multi-chromosome NIPTeR conformance fixture: make fixtures currently produces chr11-BAM. multi-chromosome synthetic BAM (24 chroms, unmapped reads, -position collisions) allow NIPTER_CONFORMANCE_BAM populated automatically CI without real patient BAM.

R/nipter_score.R — Z-score NCV scoring. R/nipter_chi.R — chi-squared overdispersion correction. R/nipter_control.R — control group construction, diagnostics, matching. WisecondorX conformance script ../../duckhts/scripts/wisecondorx_convert_conformance.py. inst/python/ directory.

title: RWisecondorX Agent Guidelines
text: BED.gz format language-agnostic handoff binning downstream analysis: 4-column WisecondorX BED: chrom, start, end, count (written bam_convert_bed()). 5-column NIPTeR BED: chrom, start, end, count, corrected_count (written nipter_bin_bam_bed()). Coordinates 0-based half-open intervals (BED convention). Chromosomes use chr prefix. files bgzipped (BGZF) tabix-indexed via Rduckhts::rduckhts_bgzip() Rduckhts::rduckhts_tabix_index(). use gzfile() external tools.

what: Convert Step: Exact SQL Replication of pysam larp/larp2
title: RWisecondorX Agent Guidelines
text: WisecondorX convert step uses streaming dedup state machine (larp/larp2). exact SQL equivalent uses DuckDB window functions FILE_OFFSET: Key subtleties reproduced exactly: Improper pairs (is_paired is_proper) invisible larp larp2. filter internal rmdup = "streaming" — intrinsic WisecondorX algorithm, user flag option. Unpaired reads update larp larp2 — hence IGNORE NULLS trick. larp never reset chromosomes — hence PARTITION . Bin assignment uses integer division pos // binsize matching Python’s int(pos / binsize). pos 0-based: subtract 1 duckhts 1-based POS dividing.
code: LAG(pos) OVER (ORDER BY file_offset)                                          -- prev_pos (larp) LAST_VALUE(CASE WHEN is_paired != 0 THEN pnext END IGNORE NULLS)              -- prev_pnext (larp2)     OVER (ORDER BY file_offset ROWS BETWEEN UNBOUNDED PRECEDING AND 1 PRECEDING)

title: RWisecondorX Agent Guidelines
text: bam_convert(), bam_convert_bed(), nipter_bin_bam(), nipter_bin_bam_bed() expose samtools-style -f/-F flag filtering: require_flags (integer bitmask, default 0L): reads (flag & require_flags) == require_flags kept. Equivalent samtools view -f. exclude_flags (integer bitmask, default 0L): reads (flag & exclude_flags) != 0 dropped. Equivalent samtools view -F. Common real-world NIPT pre-filter pattern (matches samtools view --min-MQ 40 -F 1024):
code: nipter_bin_bam("sample.dm.bam", mapq = 40L, exclude_flags = 1024L)

previous_headings: NIPTeR Layer: Known Issues and Design Decisions
what: NIPTeR’s scanBam approach is “concat and pretend single-ended”
title: RWisecondorX Agent Guidelines
text: NIPTeR’s bin_bam_sample() calls scanBam() without filtering parameters, splits read records strand (+ / -), discarding reads strand "*" (unmapped). applies unique() positions per chromosome per strand sums forward reverse counts single matrix. consequence paired-end data treated concatenated mates single-ended read pool: R1 (forward) R2 (reverse) counted independently respective start positions. paired-end library produces roughly 2× bin counts single-end library sequencing depth, mates every fragment counted. creates fundamental incompatibility: NIPTeR reference (control group) built single-end samples used test paired-end sample, vice versa. practice goes unnoticed samples cohort use library preparation, mixing single-end paired-end data produces wildly incorrect Z-scores NCV values. nipter_bin_bam() replicates behaviour (mates counted) default (rmdup = "none", flag filters), keeping compatible NIPTeR control groups built way. Users need fragment-level counts (one count per fragment, per read) pre-filter R1 using require_flags = 0x41L (paired + read1) count unique fragments — future enhancement.

previous_headings: NIPTeR Layer: Known Issues and Design Decisions
what: NIPTeR’s implicit positional dedup is similar to WisecondorX streaming dedup, but different
title: RWisecondorX Agent Guidelines
text: NIPTeR’s bin_reads() applies unique() read positions per chromosome per strand binning. effect two reads start position chromosome strand counted , twice. position-based deduplication. WisecondorX’s larp also collapses consecutive reads position. critical differences: collapse -position reads, NIPTeR’s unique() also silently merges two reads happen share start position, including distinct reads PCR duplicates. hg00106_chr11_fixture.bam fixture (1,018 paired-end reads) NIPTeR’s approach count 1,003 vs 1,018 rmdup = "none" — difference 15 reads, positions duplicate flag (0x400) set one mate.

what: Two additional bugs in NIPTeR’s scanBam call
title: RWisecondorX Agent Guidelines
text: Bug 1 — Split length mismatch BAMs unmapped reads. strands[strands != "*"] length N − U (U = unmapped reads) reads length N. R’s split() requires equal lengths, errors silently misbehaves BAM containing unmapped reads. NIPTeR implicitly requires samtools view -F 4 pre-filtering. Bug 2 — Positional dedup via unique() flag-ignorant. tabulate() counts unique positions per bin, reads. PCR duplicate unique position survives; two distinct reads position silently merged. equivalent -F 1024. Consequence conformance testing. Exact bin--bin match NIPTeR::bin_bam_sample() requires BAM unmapped reads two reads sharing start position per strand. NIPTER_CONFORMANCE_BAM env var must point BAM. rmdup = "none" closest mode counts read independently rather unique positions.
code: # NIPTeR source — bin_sample.R splitted_reads <- split(x = reads, f = droplevels(strands[strands != "*"])) # NIPTeR source — bin_sample.R / bin_reads() reads <- sapply(X = unique(reads_data_frame[min_read:max_read]),                 FUN = getbins, bin_size = bin_size) bins <- tabulate(reads, nbins = n_bins)

what: An R Port of WisecondorX for Copy Number Analysis using Duckhts
title: An R Port of WisecondorX for Copy Number Analysis using Duckhts
text: RWisecondorX R toolkit copy number analysis trisomy prediction non-invasive prenatal testing (NIPT), built top Rduckhts DuckDB. ports WisecondorX (CNV detection) NIPTeR (trisomy prediction via Z-scores, NCV, regression, chi-squared correction), following rewrites.bio principles: exact emulation upstream outputs, full credit original authors, transparency AI assistance. core convert step runs entirely R/SQL via Rduckhts Python dependency. bam_convert() returns per-bin read counts memory; bam_convert_bed() writes bgzipped, tabix-indexed BED file readable DuckDB tabix-aware tool; bam_convert_npz() serialises WisecondorX-compatible .npz via reticulate. convert implementation exactly replicates upstream larp / larp2 streaming deduplication behaviour, achieving bin--bin agreement Python implementation. NIPTeR statistical layer provides GC correction (via --fly FASTA computation, bundled tables), chi-squared overdispersion correction, chromosomal fraction Z-scores, normalised chromosome values (NCV), forward stepwise regression — operating NIPTeRSample objects produced nipter_bin_bam(). pipelines need full upstream toolchain, thin condathis wrappers cover every stage: wisecondorx_convert(), wisecondorx_newref(), wisecondorx_predict() delegate official bioconda package without requiring manual conda setup.

"none" skips deduplication entirely, matching wisecondorx convert --normdup NIPT data read depth low. "flag" uses SAM duplicate flag (0x400) BAMs already processed Picard sambamba.

title: An R Port of WisecondorX for Copy Number Analysis using Duckhts
text: full upstream WisecondorX pipeline needed, wisecondorx_convert() wraps wisecondorx convert via condathis produces .npz official Python implementation. wisecondorx_newref() wisecondorx_predict() cover remaining stages. three handle conda environment creation automatically first use. CLI wrappers expose upstream arguments documented WisecondorX README. delegate official wisecondorx bioconda package installed automatically via condathis.
code: wisecondorx_convert(   bam     = "sample.bam",   npz     = "sample.npz",   binsize = 5000L,   normdup = FALSE          # TRUE → --normdup (NIPT) ) library(RWisecondorX)  fixture_npz <- tempfile(fileext = ".npz") np <- reticulate::import("numpy", convert = FALSE)  bam_convert_npz(   bam     = fixture_bam,   npz     = fixture_npz,   binsize = 5000L,   rmdup   = "streaming",   np      = np )  c(   npz_created = file.exists(fixture_npz),   npz_size    = file.info(fixture_npz)$size ) #> npz_created    npz_size  #>           1         392 library(RWisecondorX)  reference_npz <- tempfile(fileext = ".npz") plotyfrac_png <- tempfile(fileext = ".png") output_prefix <- tempfile(pattern = "wisecondorx-")  wisecondorx_newref(   npz_files   = c(fixture_npz, fixture_npz),   output      = reference_npz,   binsize     = 5000L,   ref_binsize = 50000L,   nipt        = TRUE,   refsize     = 300L,   yfrac       = NULL,   plotyfrac   = plotyfrac_png,   cpus        = 1L )  wisecondorx_predict(   npz          = fixture_npz,   ref          = reference_npz,   output_prefix = output_prefix,   ref_binsize  = 50000L,   minrefbins   = 150L,   maskrepeats  = 5L,   zscore       = 5,   alpha        = 1e-4,   beta         = NULL,   blacklist    = NULL,   gender       = NULL,   bed          = TRUE,   plot         = FALSE,   regions      = NULL,   ylim         = NULL,   cairo        = FALSE,   seed         = 1L )

title: An R Port of WisecondorX for Copy Number Analysis using Duckhts
text: RWisecondorX also ports statistical analysis layer NIPTeR (de Weerd et al.), replacing Rsamtools Rduckhts bundled sysdata.rda tables --fly computation via rduckhts_fasta_nuc(). analysis pipeline follows NIPTeR’s workflow: bin samples, build control group, correct GC bias overdispersion, compute Z-scores NCV values trisomy prediction.

title: An R Port of WisecondorX for Copy Number Analysis using Duckhts
text: nipter_bin_bam() produces NIPTeRSample objects compatible downstream functions. Pre-filtering flags mirror samtools view -f/-F conventions, real-world NIPT pipelines mark duplicates Picard can pass exclude_flags = 1024L directly.
code: samples <- lapply(bam_files, nipter_bin_bam, binsize = 50000L)

title: An R Port of WisecondorX for Copy Number Analysis using Duckhts
text: control group reference cohort test samples scored. nipter_as_control_group() validates deduplicates input samples. nipter_diagnose_control_group() reports per-chromosome Z-scores Shapiro-Wilk normality p-values across group, useful identifying outlier samples scoring. working large cohort, nipter_match_control_group() selects closest-matching subset given test sample based chromosomal fraction distance.
code: cg <- nipter_as_control_group(samples) diag <- nipter_diagnose_control_group(cg) matched_cg <- nipter_match_control_group(test_sample, cg, n_controls = 50L)

title: An R Port of WisecondorX for Copy Number Analysis using Duckhts
text: nipter_gc_correct() adjusts bin counts GC-content bias. GC percentages computed per bin reference FASTA via rduckhts_fasta_nuc() rather static bundled tables. Two methods available: LOESS regression (default, matching NIPTeR) bin-weight normalisation. function accepts either single sample entire control group.
code: cg <- nipter_gc_correct(cg, fasta = "hg38.fa", method = "loess") test_sample <- nipter_gc_correct(test_sample, fasta = "hg38.fa")

title: An R Port of WisecondorX for Copy Number Analysis using Duckhts
text: nipter_chi_correct() identifies overdispersed bins across control group using normalised chi-squared test downweights . correction applied simultaneously test sample control group, maintaining consistency downstream scoring.
code: corrected <- nipter_chi_correct(test_sample, cg, chi_cutoff = 3.5) test_sample <- corrected$sample cg <- corrected$control_group

title: An R Port of WisecondorX for Copy Number Analysis using Duckhts
text: nipter_z_score() computes chromosomal fraction Z-score focus chromosome, testing far sample deviates control distribution. Z-score 3 conventional threshold trisomy. result includes Shapiro-Wilk p-value control distribution normality violations visible. nipter_ncv_score() computes normalised chromosome value, searches denominator chromosome set minimises control-group variance computing Z-score. robust standard Z-score control group small chromosomal fraction high variance. nipter_regression() uses forward stepwise linear regression predict focus chromosome’s fraction chromosomes, scores residual. builds multiple models train/test split selects CV (practical vs theoretical) gives conservative estimate.
code: z21 <- nipter_z_score(test_sample, cg, chromo_focus = 21) z21$sample_z_score z21$control_statistics ncv21 <- nipter_ncv_score(test_sample, cg, chromo_focus = 21) ncv21$sample_ncv_score ncv21$denominators reg21 <- nipter_regression(test_sample, cg, chromo_focus = 21) reg21$models[[1]]$z_score reg21$models[[1]]$predictors

title: Count reads per bin from a BAM or CRAM file — bam_convert
text: Core read-counting engine shared WisecondorX NIPTeR layers. Reads aligned reads BAM CRAM file returns per-bin read counts chromosomes 1-22 sex chromosomes (X mapped key "23", Y "24").

title: Count reads per bin from a BAM or CRAM file — bam_convert
code: bam_convert(   bam,   binsize = 5000L,   mapq = 1L,   require_flags = 0L,   exclude_flags = 0L,   rmdup = c("streaming", "flag", "none"),   con = NULL,   reference = NULL )

title: Count reads per bin from a BAM or CRAM file — bam_convert
text: bam Path indexed BAM CRAM file. binsize Bin size base pairs. Default 5000L (WisecondorX); use 50000L NIPTeR-style workflows. mapq Minimum mapping quality. Default 1L (WisecondorX / samtools default). Set 0L retain reads regardless MAPQ (NIPTeR). require_flags Integer bitmask. reads (FLAG & require_flags) == require_flags retained. 0L (default) imposes requirement. Example: require_flags = 0x2L keeps properly paired reads. exclude_flags Integer bitmask. Reads (FLAG & exclude_flags) != 0 dropped. 0L (default) drops nothing. Example: exclude_flags = 0xF04L drops unmapped, secondary, QC-fail supplementary reads (common samtools pre-filter). rmdup Duplicate removal strategy. "streaming" (default) applies WisecondorX larp/larp2 algorithm (also excludes improper pairs). "flag" drops reads SAM flag 0x400. "none" keeps reads pass filters. con Optional open DBI connection duckhts already loaded. NULL (default) temporary -memory DuckDB connection created. reference Optional FASTA reference path CRAM inputs.

title: Count reads per bin from a BAM or CRAM file — bam_convert
text: named list one integer vector per chromosome key ("1"– "22", "23" X, "24" Y). vector contains per-bin read counts (bin 0 = positions 0 binsize - 1). Chromosomes absent BAM NULL.

title: Count reads per bin from a BAM or CRAM file — bam_convert
text: Read filtering mirrors samtools view convention: mapq sets minimum mapping quality; require_flags bitmask flags must set (equivalent samtools view -f); exclude_flags bitmask flags must clear (equivalent samtools view -F).  Use duckhts UDF sam_flag_bits(flag) inspect named flag fields, sam_flag_has(flag, bit) test individual bits. rmdup controls duplicate removal independently flag filters: "streaming" applies WisecondorX larp/larp2 consecutive-position state machine also enforces WisecondorX improper-pair rule (paired reads properly paired excluded counting dedup state — intrinsic algorithm, flag option); "flag" additionally excludes reads SAM flag 0x400 set (Picard / sambamba pre-marked duplicates); "none" applies deduplication.

title: Count reads per bin from a BAM or CRAM file — bam_convert
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # WisecondorX defaults bins <- bam_convert("sample.bam")  # NIPTeR defaults — all mapped reads, no dedup, 50 kb bins bins <- bam_convert("sample.bam", binsize = 50000L, mapq = 0L,                     rmdup = "none")  # Pre-filtered BAM: skip unmapped + secondary + supplementary, flag dedup bins <- bam_convert("sample.bam",                     exclude_flags = bitwOr(0x4L, bitwOr(0x100L, 0x800L)),                     rmdup = "flag") } # }

title: Convert BAM/CRAM to a bgzipped BED bin-count file — bam_convert_bed
text: bam Path indexed BAM CRAM file. bed Path output .bed.gz file (created overwritten). tabix index written paste0(bed, ".tbi"). binsize Bin size base pairs (default 5000). mapq Minimum mapping quality (default 1L). require_flags Integer bitmask; reads bits set kept (samtools -f). Default 0L (requirement). exclude_flags Integer bitmask; reads bit set dropped (samtools -F). Default 0L (nothing dropped). rmdup Duplicate-removal strategy passed bam_convert(). con Optional open DBI connection duckhts already loaded. NULL (default), temporary -memory DuckDB connection created. reference Optional FASTA reference path CRAM inputs. index Logical; TRUE (default) tabix index created alongside bgzipped output.

text: Replicates NIPTeR's bin_bam_sample(): counts reads fixed-width bins across chromosomes 1-22, X, Y. original NIPTeR counts mapped reads MAPQ filter; real-world NIPT pipelines typically pre-filter samtools view --min-MQ 40 -F 1024 binning. modes supported mapq, exclude_flags, require_flags parameters.

code: nipter_bin_bam(   bam,   binsize = 50000L,   mapq = 0L,   require_flags = 0L,   exclude_flags = 0L,   rmdup = c("none", "flag"),   separate_strands = FALSE,   con = NULL,   reference = NULL )

title: Bin a BAM/CRAM file — NIPTeR style — nipter_bin_bam
text: bam Path indexed BAM CRAM file. binsize Bin size base pairs. Default 50000 (NIPTeR's fixed bin size). Must match binsize used building control group. mapq Minimum mapping quality. Default 0L (NIPTeR original: mapped reads). Set 40L match common NIPT pipeline pre-filtering (samtools view --min-MQ 40). require_flags Integer bitmask; reads bits set kept (samtools -f). Default 0L (requirement). exclude_flags Integer bitmask; reads bit set dropped (samtools -F). Default 0L. Set 1024L (0x400) exclude reads marked duplicates (samtools view -F 1024). rmdup Duplicate removal strategy. "none" (default, NIPTeR standard) "flag" (equivalent exclude_flags = 1024L; BAMs already processed Picard / sambamba). NIPTeR perform streaming deduplication. separate_strands yet implemented. Set FALSE (default). con Optional open DBI connection duckhts already loaded. reference Optional FASTA reference path CRAM inputs.

title: Bin a BAM/CRAM file — NIPTeR style — nipter_bin_bam
text: object class NIPTeRSample: named list autosomal_chromosome_reads (list one 22-row integer matrix, rows named "1"–"22", columns bins), sex_chromosome_reads (list one 2-row integer matrix, rows named "X" "Y"), correction_status_autosomal ("Uncorrected"), correction_status_sex ("Uncorrected"), sample_name.

title: Bin a BAM/CRAM file — NIPTeR style — nipter_bin_bam
text: result reshaped NIPTeRSample object whose structure parallels NIPTeR's NIPTSample: autosomal reads chromosome--bin matrix sex chromosome reads separate two-row matrix. Strand separation (separate_strands = TRUE) yet implemented; requires forward/reverse split SQL layer added NIPTeR regression layer ported.

what: Chi-squared correction for overdispersed bins — nipter_chi_correct
title: Chi-squared correction for overdispersed bins — nipter_chi_correct
text: Identifies bins control group excess variance (overdispersion) using chi-squared test downweights . reduces influence noisy bins downstream Z-scores NCV calculations.

title: Chi-squared correction for overdispersed bins — nipter_chi_correct
code: nipter_chi_correct(   sample,   control_group,   chi_cutoff = 3.5,   include_sex = FALSE )

title: Chi-squared correction for overdispersed bins — nipter_chi_correct
text: sample NIPTeRSample object (test sample). control_group NIPTeRControlGroup object. chi_cutoff Normalised chi-squared threshold. Bins (chi - df) / sqrt(2*df) > chi_cutoff corrected. Default 3.5. include_sex Logical; correct sex chromosomes well? Default FALSE. Sex chromosome bins use chi-squared weights derived autosomes.

title: Chi-squared correction for overdispersed bins — nipter_chi_correct
text: list two elements: sample corrected NIPTeRSample. control_group corrected NIPTeRControlGroup.

title: Chi-squared correction for overdispersed bins — nipter_chi_correct
text: correction applied simultaneously test sample control group samples, maintaining consistency. algorithm follows NIPTeR's chi-squared correction: control sample, scale autosomal bin counts total reads match overall mean across control samples. Compute expected count per bin (mean scaled counts). Compute chi-squared per bin: \(\chi^2 = \sum_i (expected - scaled_i)^2 / expected\). Normalise: \(z = (\chi^2 - df) / \sqrt{2 \cdot df}\) \(df = n_{controls} - 1\). bins \(z > \text{chi\_cutoff}\): divide reads \(\chi^2 / df\).

title: Chi-squared correction for overdispersed bins — nipter_chi_correct

text: object NIPTeRSample NIPTeRControlGroup. fasta Path indexed reference FASTA file (.fa/.fasta .fai index). method GC correction method: "loess" (default) "bin" (bin-weight). span LOESS smoothing parameter (used method = "loess"). Default 0.75. include_sex Logical; correct sex chromosomes (X, Y) well? Default FALSE. binsize Bin size used binning sample (default 50000L). Must match binsize sample. con Optional open DBI connection duckhts loaded. NULL, temporary connection created.

title: GC-correct a NIPTeR sample or control group — nipter_gc_correct
text: corrected copy object class. Correction status updated "Uncorrected" "GC corrected".

title: GC-correct a NIPTeR sample or control group — nipter_gc_correct
text: LOESS method (default): Fits LOESS curve read counts vs GC percentage across autosomal bins known GC non-zero reads. bin scaled median(counts) / fitted(loess), bins normalised genome-wide median. NIPTeR default method. Bin-weight method: Groups bins GC percentage (0.1\ computes mean read count per GC bucket, scales bin global_mean / bucket_mean. Faster LOESS less smooth. Sex chromosome correction (include_sex = TRUE) uses nearest-neighbour lookup autosomal LOESS curve (LOESS method) GC bucket weights (bin-weight method).

text: sample NIPTeRSample object. control_group NIPTeRControlGroup object. chromo_focus Integer; target chromosome (1-22). max_elements Maximum number denominator chromosomes try (default 5). algorithm searches combinations 1 max_elements candidate pool. exclude_chromosomes Integer vector chromosomes exclude denominator pool (default c(13, 18, 21) — trisomy chromosomes). include_chromosomes Integer vector chromosomes force-include candidate pool. Default FALSE. seed Random seed train/test split. Default NULL (seed set).

title: Regression-based Z-score for trisomy prediction — nipter_regression
text: list class "NIPTeRRegression" elements: models list n_models sublists, containing: z_score (numeric), cv (selected CV), cv_type ("practical" "theoretical"), predictors (character vector predictor chromosomes), shapiro_p_value, control_z_scores (named numeric vector). focus_chromosome Character. correction_status Character vector. sample_name Character.

title: Regression-based Z-score for trisomy prediction — nipter_regression
text: Currently supports CombinedStrands samples. SeparatedStrands variant (doubles predictor pool forward/reverse per chromosome) added separate_strands = TRUE support implemented nipter_bin_bam. algorithm: Split control group 60/40 train test sets. Compute chromosomal fractions samples. n_models models, greedily select n_predictors chromosomes maximising adjusted R-squared (forward stepwise selection). Predictors already used previous models excluded. model, fit lm(focus ~ predictors) training set, predict test set, compute: Practical CV: sd(obs/pred) / mean(obs/pred) test set. Theoretical CV: overdispersion_rate / sqrt(total_reads_focus) test sample. larger CV used (unless force_practical_cv = TRUE). Sample Z-score: (sample_ratio - 1) / cv.
